chapters.zh: 先前满有人民的城，现在何竟独坐！先前在列国中为大的，现在竟如寡妇；先前在诸省中为王后的，现在成为进贡的。
chapters.en: How doth the city sit solitary, full of people! is she become as a widow! she great among the nations, princess among the provinces, is she become tributary!

chapters.zh: 她夜间痛哭，泪流满腮；在一切所亲爱的中间没有一个安慰她的。她的朋友都以诡诈待她，成为她的仇敌。
chapters.en: She weepeth sore in the night, and her tears on her cheeks: among all her lovers she hath none to comfort : all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they are become her enemies.

chapters.zh: 犹大因遭遇苦难，又因多服劳苦就迁到外邦。她住在列国中，寻不著安息；追逼她的都在狭窄之地将她追上。
chapters.en: Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction, and because of great servitude: she dwelleth among the heathen, she findeth no rest: all her persecutors overtook her between the straits.

chapters.zh: 锡安的路径因无人来守圣节就悲伤；她的城门凄凉；她的祭司叹息；她的处女受艰难，自己也愁苦。
chapters.en: The ways of Zion do mourn, because none come to the solemn feasts: all her gates are desolate: her priests sigh, her virgins are afflicted, and she in bitterness.

chapters.zh: 她的敌人为首；她的仇敌亨通；因耶和华为她许多的罪过使她受苦；她的孩童被敌人掳去。
chapters.en: Her adversaries are the chief, her enemies prosper; for the LORD hath afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions: her children are gone into captivity before the enemy.

chapters.zh: 锡安城（原文是女子；下同）的威荣全都失去。她的首领像找不著草场的鹿；在追赶的人前无力行走。
chapters.en: And from the daughter of Zion all her beauty is departed: her princes are become like harts find no pasture, and they are gone without strength before the pursuer.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷在困苦窘迫之时，就追想古时一切的乐境。她百姓落在敌人手中，无人救济；敌人看见，就因她的荒凉嗤笑。
chapters.en: Jerusalem remembered in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that she had in the days of old, when her people fell into the hand of the enemy, and none did help her: the adversaries saw her, did mock at her sabbaths.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷大大犯罪，所以成为不洁之物；素来尊敬她的，见她赤露就都藐视她；她自己也叹息退后。
chapters.en: Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore she is removed: all that honoured her despise her, because they have seen her nakedness: yea, she sigheth, and turneth backward.

chapters.zh: 她的污秽是在衣襟上；她不思想自己的结局，所以非常地败落，无人安慰她。她说：耶和华啊，求你看我的苦难，因为仇敌夸大。
chapters.en: Her filthiness in her skirts; she remembereth not her last end; therefore she came down wonderfully: she had no comforter. O LORD, behold my affliction: for the enemy hath magnified .

chapters.zh: 敌人伸手，夺取她的美物；她眼见外邦人进入她的圣所 ─ 论这外邦人，你曾吩咐不可入你的会中。
chapters.en: The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her pleasant things: for she hath seen the heathen entered into her sanctuary, whom thou didst command they should not enter into thy congregation.

chapters.zh: 她的民都叹息，寻求食物；他们用美物换粮食，要救性命。他们说：耶和华啊，求你观看，因为我甚是卑贱。
chapters.en: All her people sigh, they seek bread; they have given their pleasant things for meat to relieve the soul: see, O LORD, and consider; for I am become vile.

chapters.zh: 你们一切过路的人哪，这事你们不介意么？你们要观看：有像这临到我的痛苦没有 ─ 就是耶和华在他发烈怒的日子使我所受的苦？
chapters.en: nothing to you, all ye that pass by? behold, and see if there be any sorrow like unto my sorrow, which is done unto me, wherewith the LORD hath afflicted in the day of his fierce anger.

chapters.zh: 他从高天使火进入我的骨头，克制了我；他铺下网罗，绊我的脚，使我转回；他使我终日凄凉发昏。
chapters.en: From above hath he sent fire into my bones, and it prevaileth against them: he hath spread a net for my feet, he hath turned me back: he hath made me desolate faint all the day.

chapters.zh: 我罪过的轭是他手所绑的，犹如轭绳缚在我颈项上；他使我的力量衰败。主将我交在我所不能敌挡的人手中。
chapters.en: The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed, come up upon my neck: he hath made my strength to fall, the Lord hath delivered me into hands, I am not able to rise up.

chapters.zh: 主轻弃我中间的一切勇士，招聚多人（原文是大会）攻击我，要压碎我的少年人。主将犹大居民踹下，像在酒醡中一样。
chapters.en: The Lord hath trodden under foot all my mighty in the midst of me: he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men: the Lord hath trodden the virgin, the daughter of Judah, in a winepress.

chapters.zh: 我因这些事哭泣；我眼泪汪汪；因为那当安慰我、救我性命的，离我甚远。我的儿女孤苦，因为仇敌得了胜。
chapters.en: For these I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.

chapters.zh: 锡安举手，无人安慰。耶和华论雅各已经出令，使四围的人作他仇敌；耶路撒冷在他们中间像不洁之物。
chapters.en: Zion spreadeth forth her hands, none to comfort her: the LORD hath commanded concerning Jacob, his adversaries round about him: Jerusalem is as a menstruous woman among them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是公义的！他这样待我，是因我违背他的命令。众民哪，请听我的话，看我的痛苦；我的处女和少年人都被掳去。
chapters.en: The LORD is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment: hear, I pray you, all people, and behold my sorrow: my virgins and my young men are gone into captivity.

chapters.zh: 我招呼我所亲爱的，他们却愚弄我。我的祭司和长老正寻求食物、救性命的时候，就在城中绝气。
chapters.en: I called for my lovers, they deceived me: my priests and mine elders gave up the ghost in the city, while they sought their meat to relieve their souls.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你观看，因为我在急难中。我心肠扰乱；我心在我里面翻转，因我大大悖逆。在外，刀剑使人丧子；在家，犹如死亡。
chapters.en: Behold, O LORD; for I in distress: my bowels are troubled; mine heart is turned within me; for I have grievously rebelled: abroad the sword bereaveth, at home as death.

chapters.zh: 听见我叹息的有人；安慰我的却无人！我的仇敌都听见我所遭的患难；因你做这事，他们都喜乐。你必使你报告的日子来到，他们就像我一样。
chapters.en: They have heard that I sigh: none to comfort me: all mine enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done : thou wilt bring the day thou hast called, and they shall be like unto me.

chapters.zh: 愿他们的恶行都呈在你面前；你怎样因我的一切罪过待我，求你照样待他们；因我叹息甚多，心中发昏。
chapters.en: Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs many, and my heart faint.

chapters.zh: 主何竟发怒，使黑云遮蔽锡安城！他将以色列的华美从天扔在地上；在他发怒的日子并不记念自己的脚凳。
chapters.en: How hath the Lord covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in his anger, cast down from heaven unto the earth the beauty of Israel, and remembered not his footstool in the day of his anger!

chapters.zh: 主吞灭雅各一切的住处，并不顾惜。他发怒倾覆犹大民的保障，使这保障坍倒在地；他辱没这国和其中的首领。
chapters.en: The Lord hath swallowed up all the habitations of Jacob, and hath not pitied: he hath thrown down in his wrath the strong holds of the daughter of Judah; he hath brought down to the ground: he hath polluted the kingdom and the princes thereof.

chapters.zh: 他发烈怒，把以色列的角全然砍断，在仇敌面前收回右手。他像火焰四围吞灭，将雅各烧毁。
chapters.en: He hath cut off in fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy, and he burned against Jacob like a flaming fire, devoureth round about.

chapters.zh: 他张弓好像仇敌；他站著举起右手，如同敌人将悦人眼目的，尽行杀戮。在锡安百姓的帐棚上倒出他的忿怒，像火一样。
chapters.en: He hath bent his bow like an enemy: he stood with his right hand as an adversary, and slew all pleasant to the eye in the tabernacle of the daughter of Zion: he poured out his fury like fire.

chapters.zh: 主如仇敌吞灭以色列和锡安的一切宫殿，拆毁百姓的保障；在犹大民中加增悲伤哭号。
chapters.en: The Lord was as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel, he hath swallowed up all her palaces: he hath destroyed his strong holds, and hath increased in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.

chapters.zh: 他强取自己的帐幕，好像是园中的窝棚，毁坏他的聚会之处。耶和华使圣节和安息日在锡安都被忘记，又在怒气的愤恨中藐视君王和祭司。
chapters.en: And he hath violently taken away his tabernacle, as a garden: he hath destroyed his places of the assembly: the LORD hath caused the solemn feasts and sabbaths to be forgotten in Zion, and hath despised in the indignation of his anger the king and the priest.

chapters.zh: 耶和华丢弃自己的祭坛，憎恶自己的圣所，将宫殿的墙垣交付仇敌。他们在耶和华的殿中喧嚷，像在圣会之日一样。
chapters.en: The Lord hath cast off his altar, he hath abhorred his sanctuary, he hath given up into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces; they have made a noise in the house of the LORD, as in the day of a solemn feast.

chapters.zh: 耶和华定意拆毁锡安的城墙；他拉了准绳，不将手收回，定要毁灭。他使外郭和城墙都悲哀，一同衰败。
chapters.en: The LORD hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out a line, he hath not withdrawn his hand from destroying: therefore he made the rampart and the wall to lament; they languished together.

chapters.zh: 锡安的门都陷入地内；主将她的门闩毁坏，折断。她的君王和首领落在没有律法的列国中；她的先知不得见耶和华的异象。
chapters.en: Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars: her king and her princes among the Gentiles: the law no ; her prophets also find no vision from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 锡安城的长老坐在地上默默无声；他们扬起尘土落在头上，腰束麻布；耶路撒冷的处女垂头至地。
chapters.en: The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground, keep silence: they have cast up dust upon their heads; they have girded themselves with sackcloth: the virgins of Jerusalem hang down their heads to the ground.

chapters.zh: 我眼中流泪，以致失明，我的心肠扰乱，肝胆涂地，都因我众民遭毁灭，又因孩童和吃奶的在城内街上发昏。
chapters.en: Mine eyes do fail with tears, my bowels are troubled, my liver is poured upon the earth, for the destruction of the daughter of my people; because the children and the sucklings swoon in the streets of the city.

chapters.zh: 那时，他们在城内街上发昏，好像受伤的，在母亲的怀里，将要丧命；对母亲说：谷、酒在哪里呢？
chapters.en: They say to their mothers, Where corn and wine? when they swooned as the wounded in the streets of the city, when their soul was poured out into their mothers' bosom.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的民哪，我可用甚么向你证明呢？我可用甚么与你相比呢？锡安的民哪，我可拿甚么和你比较，好安慰你呢？因为你的裂口大如海，谁能医治你呢？
chapters.en: What thing shall I take to witness for thee? what thing shall I liken to thee, O daughter of Jerusalem? what shall I equal to thee, that I may comfort thee, O virgin daughter of Zion? for thy breach great like the sea: who can heal thee?

chapters.zh: 你的先知为你见虚假和愚昧的异象，并没有显露你的罪孽，使你被掳的归回；却为你见虚假的默示和使你被赶出本境的缘故。
chapters.en: Thy prophets have seen vain and foolish things for thee: and they have not discovered thine iniquity, to turn away thy captivity; but have seen for thee false burdens and causes of banishment.

chapters.zh: 凡过路的都向你拍掌。他们向耶路撒冷城嗤笑，摇头，说：难道人所称为全美的，称为全地所喜悦的，就是这城吗？
chapters.en: All that pass by clap hands at thee; they hiss and wag their head at the daughter of Jerusalem, this the city that call The perfection of beauty, The joy of the whole earth?

chapters.zh: 你的仇敌都向你大大张口；他们嗤笑，又切齿说：我们吞灭她。这真是我们所盼望的日子临到了！我们亲眼看见了！
chapters.en: All thine enemies have opened their mouth against thee: they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say, We have swallowed up: certainly this the day that we looked for; we have found, we have seen .

chapters.zh: 耶和华成就了他所定的，应验了他古时所命定的。他倾覆了，并不顾惜，使你的仇敌向你夸耀；使你敌人的角也被高举。
chapters.en: The LORD hath done which he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word that he had commanded in the days of old: he hath thrown down, and hath not pitied: and he hath caused enemy to rejoice over thee, he hath set up the horn of thine adversaries.

chapters.zh: 锡安民的心哀求主。锡安的城墙啊，愿你流泪如河，昼夜不息；愿你眼中的瞳人泪流不止。
chapters.en: Their heart cried unto the Lord, O wall of the daughter of Zion, let tears run down like a river day and night: give thyself no rest; let not the apple of thine eye cease.

chapters.zh: 夜间，每逢交更的时候要起来呼喊，在主面前倾心如水。你的孩童在各市口上受饿发昏；你要为他们的性命向主举手祷告。
chapters.en: Arise, cry out in the night: in the beginning of the watches pour out thine heart like water before the face of the Lord: lift up thy hands toward him for the life of thy young children, that faint for hunger in the top of every street.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你观看！见你向谁这样行？妇人岂可吃自己所生育手里所摇弄的婴孩吗？祭司和先知岂可在主的圣所中被杀戮吗？
chapters.en: Behold, O LORD, and consider to whom thou hast done this. Shall the women eat their fruit, children of a span long? shall the priest and the prophet be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?

chapters.zh: 少年人和老年人都在街上躺卧；我的处女和壮丁都倒在刀下；你发怒的日子杀死他们。你杀了，并不顾惜。
chapters.en: The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets: my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword; thou hast slain in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed, not pitied.

chapters.zh: 你招聚四围惊吓我的，像在大会的日子招聚人一样。耶和华发怒的日子，无人逃脱，无人存留。我所摇弄所养育的婴孩，仇敌都杀净了。
chapters.en: Thou hast called as in a solemn day my terrors round about, so that in the day of the LORD'S anger none escaped nor remained: those that I have swaddled and brought up hath mine enemy consumed.

chapters.zh: 我是因耶和华忿怒的杖，遭遇困苦的人。
chapters.en: I the man hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.

chapters.zh: 他引导我，使我行在黑暗中，不行在光明里。
chapters.en: He hath led me, and brought darkness, but not light.

chapters.zh: 他真是终日再三反手攻击我。
chapters.en: Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand all the day.

chapters.zh: 他使我的皮肉枯干；他折断（或译：压伤）我的骨头。
chapters.en: My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.

chapters.zh: 他筑垒攻击我，用苦楚（原文是苦胆）和艰难围困我。
chapters.en: He hath builded against me, and compassed with gall and travail.

chapters.zh: 他使我住在幽暗之处，像死了许久的人一样。
chapters.en: He hath set me in dark places, as dead of old.

chapters.zh: 他用篱笆围住我，使我不能出去；他使我的铜炼沉重。
chapters.en: He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.

chapters.zh: 我哀号求救；他使我的祷告不得上达。
chapters.en: Also when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.

chapters.zh: 他用凿过的石头挡住我的道；他使我的路弯曲。
chapters.en: He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.

chapters.zh: 他向我如熊埋伏，如狮子在隐密处。
chapters.en: He unto me a bear lying in wait, a lion in secret places.

chapters.zh: 他使我转离正路，将我撕碎，使我凄凉。
chapters.en: He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.

chapters.zh: 他张弓将我当作箭靶子。
chapters.en: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.

chapters.zh: 他把箭袋中的箭射入我的肺腑。
chapters.en: He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.

chapters.zh: 我成了众民的笑话；他们终日以我为歌曲。
chapters.en: I was a derision to all my people; their song all the day.

chapters.zh: 他用苦楚充满我，使我饱用茵 𫈟。
chapters.en: He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.

chapters.zh: 他又用沙石碜断我的牙，用灰尘将我蒙蔽。
chapters.en: He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.

chapters.zh: 你使我远离平安，我忘记好处。
chapters.en: And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.

chapters.zh: 我就说：我的力量衰败；我在耶和华那里毫无指望！
chapters.en: And I said, My strength and my hope is perished from the LORD:

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你记念我如茵 𫈟 和苦胆的困苦窘迫。
chapters.en: Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.

chapters.zh: 我心想念这些，就在里面忧闷。
chapters.en: My soul hath still in remembrance, and is humbled in me.

chapters.zh: 我想起这事，心里就有指望。
chapters.en: This I recall to my mind, therefore have I hope.

chapters.zh: 我们不致消灭，是出于耶和华诸般的慈爱；是因他的怜悯不致断绝。
chapters.en: the LORD'S mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.

chapters.zh: 每早晨，这都是新的；你的诚实极其广大！
chapters.en: new every morning: great thy faithfulness.

chapters.zh: 我心里说：耶和华是我的分，因此，我要仰望他。
chapters.en: The LORD my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.

chapters.zh: 凡等候耶和华，心里寻求他的，耶和华必施恩给他。
chapters.en: The LORD good unto them that wait for him, to the soul seeketh him.

chapters.zh: 人仰望耶和华，静默等候他的救恩，这原是好的。
chapters.en: good that should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人在幼年负轭，这原是好的。
chapters.en: good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.

chapters.zh: 他当独坐无言，因为这是耶和华加在他身上的。
chapters.en: He sitteth alone and keepeth silence, because he hath borne upon him.

chapters.zh: 他当口贴尘埃，或者有指望。
chapters.en: He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.

chapters.zh: 他当由人打他的腮颊，要满受凌辱。
chapters.en: He giveth cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.

chapters.zh: 因为主必不永远丢弃人。
chapters.en: For the Lord will not cast off for ever:

chapters.zh: 主虽使人忧愁，还要照他诸般的慈爱发怜悯。
chapters.en: But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.

chapters.zh: 因他并不甘心使人受苦，使人忧愁。
chapters.en: For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.

chapters.zh: 人将世上被囚的踹（原文是压）在脚下，
chapters.en: To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth,

chapters.zh: 或在至高者面前屈枉人，
chapters.en: To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High,

chapters.zh: 或在人的讼事上颠倒是非，这都是主看不上的。
chapters.en: To subvert a man in his cause, the Lord approveth not.

chapters.zh: 除非主命定，谁能说成就成呢？
chapters.en: Who he saith, and it cometh to pass, the Lord commandeth not?

chapters.zh: 祸福不都出于至高者的口吗？
chapters.en: Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?

chapters.zh: 活人因自己的罪受罚，为何发怨言呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?

chapters.zh: 我们当深深考察自己的行为，再归向耶和华。
chapters.en: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我们当诚心向天上的神举手祷告。
chapters.en: Let us lift up our heart with hands unto God in the heavens.

chapters.zh: 我们犯罪背逆，你并不赦免。
chapters.en: We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.

chapters.zh: 你自被怒气遮蔽，追赶我们；你施行杀戮，并不顾惜。
chapters.en: Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.

chapters.zh: 你以黑云遮蔽自己，以致祷告不得透入。
chapters.en: Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that prayer should not pass through.

chapters.zh: 你使我们在万民中成为污秽和渣滓。
chapters.en: Thou hast made us the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.

chapters.zh: 我们的仇敌都向我们大大张口。
chapters.en: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.

chapters.zh: 恐惧和陷坑，残害和毁灭，都临近我们。
chapters.en: Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.

chapters.zh: 因我众民遭的毁灭，我就眼泪下流如河。
chapters.en: Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.

chapters.zh: 我的眼多多流泪，总不止息，
chapters.en: Mine eye trickleth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission,

chapters.zh: 直等耶和华垂顾，从天观看。
chapters.en: Till the LORD look down, and behold from heaven.

chapters.zh: 因我本城的众民，我的眼，使我的心伤痛。
chapters.en: Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.

chapters.zh: 无故与我为仇的追逼我，像追雀鸟一样。
chapters.en: Mine enemies chased me sore, like a bird, without cause.

chapters.zh: 他们使我的命在牢狱中断绝，并将一块石头抛在我身上。
chapters.en: They have cut off my life in the dungeon, and cast a stone upon me.

chapters.zh: 众水流过我头，我说：我命断绝了！
chapters.en: Waters flowed over mine head; I said, I am cut off.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我从深牢中求告你的名。
chapters.en: I called upon thy name, O LORD, out of the low dungeon.

chapters.zh: 你曾听见我的声音；我求你解救，你不要掩耳不听。
chapters.en: Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.

chapters.zh: 我求告你的日子，你临近我，说：不要惧怕！
chapters.en: Thou drewest near in the day I called upon thee: thou saidst, Fear not.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你伸明了我的冤；你救赎了我的命。
chapters.en: O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你见了我受的委屈；求你为我伸冤。
chapters.en: O LORD, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.

chapters.zh: 他们仇恨我，谋害我，你都看见了。
chapters.en: Thou hast seen all their vengeance all their imaginations against me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你听见他们辱骂我的话，知道他们向我所设的计，
chapters.en: Thou hast heard their reproach, O LORD, all their imaginations against me;

chapters.zh: 并那些起来攻击我的人口中所说的话，以及终日向我所设的计谋。
chapters.en: The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.

chapters.zh: 求你观看，他们坐下，起来，都以我为歌曲。
chapters.en: Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I their musick.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你要按著他们手所做的向他们施行报应。
chapters.en: Render unto them a recompence, O LORD, according to the work of their hands.

chapters.zh: 你要使他们心里刚硬，使你的咒诅临到他们。
chapters.en: Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.

chapters.zh: 你要发怒追赶他们，从耶和华的天下除灭他们。
chapters.en: Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 黄金何其失光！纯金何其变色！圣所的石头倒在各市口上。
chapters.en: How is the gold become dim! is the most fine gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary are poured out in the top of every street.

chapters.zh: 锡安宝贵的众子好比精金，现在何竟算为窑匠手所做的瓦瓶？
chapters.en: The precious sons of Zion, comparable to fine gold, how are they esteemed as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!

chapters.zh: 野狗尚且把奶乳哺其子，我民的妇人倒成为残忍，好像旷野的鸵鸟一般。
chapters.en: Even the sea monsters draw out the breast, they give suck to their young ones: the daughter of my people cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 吃奶孩子的舌头因干渴贴住上膛；孩童求饼，无人擘给他们。
chapters.en: The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread, no man breaketh unto them.

chapters.zh: 素来吃美好食物的，现今在街上变为孤寒；素来卧朱红褥子的，现今躺卧粪堆。
chapters.en: They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.

chapters.zh: 都因我众民的罪孽比所多玛的罪还大；所多玛虽然无人加手于他，还是转眼之间被倾覆。
chapters.en: For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom, that was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands stayed on her.

chapters.zh: 锡安的贵胄素来比雪纯净，比奶更白；他们的身体比红宝玉（或译：珊瑚）更红，像光润的蓝宝石一样。
chapters.en: Her Nazarites were purer than snow, they were whiter than milk, they were more ruddy in body than rubies, their polishing of sapphire:

chapters.zh: 现在他们的面貌比煤炭更黑，以致在街上无人认识；他们的皮肤紧贴骨头，枯干如同槁木。
chapters.en: Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered, it is become like a stick.

chapters.zh: 饿死的不如被刀杀的，因为这是缺了田间的土产，就身体衰弱，渐渐消灭。
chapters.en: slain with the sword are better than slain with hunger: for these pine away, stricken through for the fruits of the field.

chapters.zh: 慈悲的妇人，当我众民被毁灭的时候，亲手煮自己的儿女作为食物。
chapters.en: The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华发怒成就他所定的，倒出他的烈怒；在锡安使火著起，烧毁锡安的根基。
chapters.en: The LORD hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger, and hath kindled a fire in Zion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.

chapters.zh: 地上的君王和世上的居民都不信敌人和仇敌能进耶路撒冷的城门。
chapters.en: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world, would not have believed that the adversary and the enemy should have entered into the gates of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 这都因他先知的罪恶和祭司的罪孽；他们在城中流了义人的血。
chapters.en: For the sins of her prophets, the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her,

chapters.zh: 他们在街上如瞎子乱走，又被血玷污，以致人不能摸他们的衣服。
chapters.en: They have wandered blind in the streets, they have polluted themselves with blood, so that men could not touch their garments.

chapters.zh: 人向他们喊著说：！不洁净的，躲开，躲开！不要挨近我！他们逃走飘流的时候，列国中有人说：他们不可仍在这里寄居。
chapters.en: They cried unto them, Depart ye; unclean; depart, depart, touch not: when they fled away and wandered, they said among the heathen, They shall no more sojourn .

chapters.zh: 耶和华发怒，将他们分散，不再眷顾他们；人不重看祭司，也不厚待长老。
chapters.en: The anger of the LORD hath divided them; he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests, they favoured not the elders.

chapters.zh: 我们仰望人来帮助，以致眼目失明，还是枉然；我们所盼望的，竟盼望一个不能救人的国！
chapters.en: As for us, our eyes as yet failed for our vain help: in our watching we have watched for a nation could not save .

chapters.zh: 仇敌追赶我们的脚步像打猎的，以致我们不敢在自己的街上行走。我们的结局临近；我们的日子满足；我们的结局来到了。
chapters.en: They hunt our steps, that we cannot go in our streets: our end is near, our days are fulfilled; for our end is come.

chapters.zh: 追赶我们的比空中的鹰更快；他们在山上追逼我们，在旷野埋伏，等候我们。
chapters.en: Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的受膏者好比我们鼻中的气，在他们的坑中被捉住；我们曾论到他说：我们必在他荫下，在列国中存活。
chapters.en: The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the LORD, was taken in their pits, of whom we said, Under his shadow we shall live among the heathen.

chapters.zh: 住乌斯地的以东民哪，只管欢喜快乐；苦杯也必传到你那里；你必喝醉，以致露体。
chapters.en: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Uz; the cup also shall pass through unto thee: thou shalt be drunken, and shalt make thyself naked.

chapters.zh: 锡安的民哪，你罪孽的刑罚受足了，耶和华必不使你再被掳去。以东的民哪，他必追讨你的罪孽，显露你的罪恶。
chapters.en: The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Zion; he will no more carry thee away into captivity: he will visit thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom; he will discover thy sins.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你记念我们所遭遇的事，观看我们所受的凌辱。
chapters.en: Remember, O LORD, what is come upon us: consider, and behold our reproach.

chapters.zh: 我们的产业归与外邦人；我们的房屋归与外路人。
chapters.en: Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.

chapters.zh: 我们是无父的孤儿；我们的母亲好像寡妇。
chapters.en: We are orphans and fatherless, our mothers as widows.

chapters.zh: 我们出钱才得水喝；我们的柴是人卖给我们的。
chapters.en: We have drunken our water for money; our wood is sold unto us.

chapters.zh: 追赶我们的，到了我们的颈项上；我们疲乏不得歇息。
chapters.en: Our necks under persecution: we labour, have no rest.

chapters.zh: 我们投降埃及人和亚述人，为要得粮吃饱。
chapters.en: We have given the hand the Egyptians, the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.

chapters.zh: 我们列祖犯罪，而今不在了；我们担当他们的罪孽。
chapters.en: Our fathers have sinned, not; and we have borne their iniquities.

chapters.zh: 奴仆辖制我们，无人救我们脱离他们的手。
chapters.en: Servants have ruled over us: none that doth deliver out of their hand.

chapters.zh: 因为旷野的刀剑，我们冒著险才得粮食。
chapters.en: We gat our bread with our lives because of the sword of the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 因饥饿燥热，我们的皮肤就黑如炉。
chapters.en: Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.

chapters.zh: 敌人在锡安玷污妇人，在犹大的城邑玷污处女。
chapters.en: They ravished the women in Zion, the maids in the cities of Judah.

chapters.zh: 他们吊起首领的手，也不尊敬老人的面。
chapters.en: Princes are hanged up by their hand: the faces of elders were not honoured.

chapters.zh: 少年人扛磨石，孩童背木柴，都绊跌了。
chapters.en: They took the young men to grind, and the children fell under the wood.

chapters.zh: 老年人在城门口断绝；少年人不再作乐。
chapters.en: The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their musick.

chapters.zh: 我们心中的快乐止息，跳舞变为悲哀。
chapters.en: The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.

chapters.zh: 冠冕从我们的头上落下；我们犯罪了，我们有祸了！
chapters.en: The crown is fallen our head: woe unto us, that we have sinned!

chapters.zh: 这些事我们心里发昏，我们的眼睛昏花。
chapters.en: For this our heart is faint; for these our eyes are dim.

chapters.zh: 锡安山荒凉，野狗（或译：狐狸）行在其上。
chapters.en: Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate, the foxes walk upon it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你存到永远；你的宝座存到万代。
chapters.en: Thou, O LORD, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.

chapters.zh: 你为何永远忘记我们？为何许久离弃我们？
chapters.en: Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, forsake us so long time?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你使我们向你回转，我们便得回转。求你复新我们的日子，像古时一样。
chapters.en: Turn thou us unto thee, O LORD, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.

chapters.zh: 你竟全然弃绝我们，向我们大发烈怒？
chapters.en: But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
